summary: A Jest test in `test/config/get.ts` failed because `pnpm config get @my-org:registry` returned a URL with a trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com/`) while the test expected no trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com`). This is a test assertion failure caused by a behavior change in how pnpm normalizes registry URLs.
root_cause: The test 'pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc' in test/config/get.ts:32 asserts that `pnpm config get @my-org:registry` outputs `https://my-org.registry.example.com` (no trailing slash), but the actual output is `https://my-org.registry.example.com/` (with a trailing slash), indicating pnpm now appends a trailing slash to registry URLs.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.reason: Identifies the specific test file that failed.

evidence.quote: ● pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc
evidence.reason: Names the specific test case that failed.
Shows the exact assertion mismatch: expected no trailing slash but received one, which is the direct cause of the test failure.
Pinpoints the exact file and line number of the failing assertion.

evidence.quote:
Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
Tests:       1 failed, 9 skipped, 475 passed, 485 total
evidence.reason: Confirms exactly 1 test suite and 1 test failed in the overall run.

suggested_fix: Update the test expectation in test/config/get.ts:32 to accept the trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com/`), or fix the pnpm config get command to strip trailing slashes from registry URLs before outputting them, depending on which behavior is intended.
